description.en: Glob the given relative pattern in the directory represented by this path, yielding all matching files (of any kind): >>> sorted(Path('.').glob('*.py')) [PosixPath('pathlib.py'), PosixPath('setup.py'), PosixPath('test_pathlib.py')] >>> sorted(Path('.').glob('*/*.py')) [PosixPath('docs/conf.py')] >>> sorted(Path('.').glob('**/*.py')) [PosixPath('build/lib/pathlib.py'), PosixPath('docs/conf.py'), PosixPath('pathlib.py'), PosixPath('setup.py'), PosixPath('test_pathlib.py')] Note The paths are re...

excerpt.es: Glob the given relative pattern in the directory represented by this path, yielding all matching files (of any kind): >>> sorted(Path('.').glob('*.py')) [PosixPath('pathlib.py'), PosixPath('setup.py')...
excerpt.en: Glob the given relative pattern in the directory represented by this path, yielding all matching files (of any kind): >>> sorted(Path('.').glob('*.py')) [PosixPath('pathlib.py'), PosixPath('setup.py')...

description.en: Return the name of the group owning the file. KeyError is raised if the file’s group identifier (GID) isn’t found in the system database. This method normally follows symlinks; to get the group of the symlink, add the argument follow_symlinks=False. Changed in version 3.13: Raises UnsupportedOperation if the grp module is not available. In earlier versions, NotImplementedError was raised. Changed in version 3.13: The follow_symlinks parameter was added.
